body.title: agent_datacenter: installer bootstrap paradox — design call and agentctl init full spec
body.proposal: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.description:
Resolve the installer bootstrap paradox and produce the full agentctl init spec for production (not the Phase 1 stub).

Problem: T-adc-portable-bootstrap (Phase 1) delivers a basic agentctl init for development. This ticket (Phase 4) locks the production installer story: what's the minimal bootstrap that lives outside the datacenter to get v1.0 running?

Proposed shape:
- Review T-adc-portable-bootstrap result: what did agentctl init actually ship?
- Gap analysis: what does a production install need that the dev init lacks?
- Make the design call: a pip install + shell script is sufficient, OR a dedicated installer device is needed
- Write docs/decisions/installer-bootstrap-v1.md with final decision
- Update T-adc-installer-bootstrap-call (Phase 0 doc) to point to this final decision

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/docs/decisions/installer-bootstrap-v1.md (new)

Design rules: None — design doc only

Scope boundary:
IN: design call doc, gap analysis vs Phase 1 portable-bootstrap
OUT: any installer code changes (those would be a follow-up ticket)

Test plan:
- docs/decisions/installer-bootstrap-v1.md exists with clear decision stated
